question:
Will the highest temperature in New York City be between 51-52°F on November 23?

Description: This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the LaGuardia Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 23 Nov '25.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the LaGuardia Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ny/new-york-city/KLGA.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

evidences.title: Will it snow in NY on Thanksgiving 2025? See the super early forecast
evidences.content: In short: it depends on where you live in New York. Western New York is predicted to be mild this winter, with below-average precipitation and a near normal amount of snow, according to the Old Farmer's Almanac. The northeastern part of the state expects normal, but "lighter-than-average" snowfall, the Southern Tier is predicted to have a colder than usual winter with below normal snowfall, and New York City and Long Island are predicted to have milder and drier weather, also with below normal snowfall. Average snowfall totals in New York can vary greatly from 25 to 31 inches in the New York City area to 95 to 127 inches in western and central New York, according to the National Weather Service. [...] In New York, the severity of the season will vary based on where you live. In some regions — particularly central New York and the Southern Tier — are expected to see a colder-than-normal winter. Other parts of the state, including western New York, the Capital Region, Adirondacks, Hudson Valley and New York City/Long Island, are forecasted to experience slightly milder-than-usual temperatures. ## How much snow will New York get in 2025-26?
